data.title: Did You Suffer Losses in BellRing Brands, Inc. (BRBR)? Contact Levi & Korsinsky About Securities Fraud Claims
data.description:
New York, New York--(Newsfile Corp. - July 25, 2025) - Levi & Korsinsky notifies investors that it has commenced an investigation of BellRing Brands, Inc. ("BellRing Brands, Inc.") concerning possible violations of federal securities laws.

On May 6, 2025, BellRing Brands during its second quarter of 2025 earnings call revealed that certain customers were now choosing to "optimize" their inventories by lowering "their weeks of supply on hand," which would slow sales growth in the third quarter to "low-single-digits."

Following this news, the price of BellRing Brands fell by nearly 19% on the same day. To obtain additional information, go to:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/bellring-brands-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=158229&wire=5&utm_campaign=65

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. either via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or by telephone at (212)363-7500.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

CONTACT:

Levi & Korsinsky, LLP

Joseph E. Levi, Esq.

Ed Korsinsky, Esq.

33 Whitehall Street, 17th Floor

New York, NY 10004

jlevi@levikorsinsky.com

Tel: (212)363-7500

Fax: (212)363-7171

https://zlk.com/

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/260156

data.title: Front Month ICE Brent Crude Fell 1.21% This Week to Settle at $68.44 — Data Talk
data.description:
Front Month ICE Brent Crude for Sept. delivery lost 84 cents per barrel, or 1.21% to $68.44 this week

  • Down for two consecutive weeks
  • Down $1.92 or 2.73% over the last two weeks
  • Largest two week net and percentage decline since the week ending July 4, 2025
  • Today it is down 74.00 cents or 1.07%
  • Largest one day dollar and percentage decline since Monday, July 14, 2025
  • Down eight of the past 10 sessions
  • Lowest settlement value since Friday, July 4, 2025
  • Off 16.84% from its 52-week high of $82.30 hit Monday, Aug. 12, 2024
  • Up 13.63% from its 52-week low of $60.23 hit Monday, May 5, 2025
  • Down 15.64% from 52 weeks ago
  • Off 16.57% from its 2025 settlement high of $82.03 hit Wednesday, Jan. 15, 2025
  • Up 13.63% from its 2025 settlement low of $60.23 hit Monday, May 5, 2025
  • Off 53.15% from its record high of $146.08 hit Thursday, July 3, 2008
  • Month-to-date it is up 1.23%
  • Year-to-date it is down $6.20 or 8.31%

All prices are calculated based on the settlement price of the current front month contract.

Source: Dow Jones Market Data

data.title: Intel to separate networking unit as new CEO Tan overhauls business
data.description:
Intel is planning to separate its networking and communications unit into a stand-alone company and has begun the process of identifying investors, the chipmaker said on Friday, as new CEO Lip-Bu Tan looks to streamline its operations.

Tan's plan to rejuvenate the once-iconic chipmaker focuses on shedding non-core assets and reducing expenses by scaling back major investments and cutting workforce numbers.

Reuters reported in May that Intel was considering divesting its network and edge businesses, previously called NEX in its financial reports.

"Like Altera, we will remain an anchor investor enabling us to benefit from future upside as we position the business for future growth," the company said in an emailed statement.

In April, Intel agreed to sell a majority stake in its Altera programmable chip business to buyout firm Silver Lake at a valuation of about $8.75 billion, or nearly half of what it had paid for the business in 2015.

Tan has been given the challenge of revitalizing the chipmaker after years of missteps and high-capital manufacturing strategies led to growing losses, all while the company grapples with establishing a foothold in the burgeoning AI market.

Intel's shares were down 9% on Friday after the chipmaker warned of exiting chip manufacturing if it failed to secure a major customer. It also reported a surprise second-quarter adjusted loss and forecast a bigger-than-expected loss in the third quarter.

In the latest first quarter, Intel made NEX a part of its data center and PC group and does not report its results as a separate segment.

The unit, which makes chips for telecom equipment, generated revenue of $5.8 billion in 2024, securities filings show. That constituted about 11% of the company's total sales.

data.title: Bridgewater Bancshares Is Maintained at Buy by DA Davidson
Wall Street gains, dollar firms ahead of a big week for market risk
data.description:
Key points:
  • Next week ahead includes tariff deadline Fed meeting, payrolls data
  • Amazon, Apple, Meta, Microsoft report next week
  • U.S. Treasury yields rise ahead of data-heavy week
  • Oil prices decline on demand outlook and Venezuela supply concerns

U.S. stocks and the dollar firmed on Friday as investors girded themselves for the week ahead, which includes a Federal Reserve policy meeting, crucial corporate results and U.S. President Donald Trump's August 1 deadline for negotiating trade deals.

"There’s increasing confidence that the economy won't be derailed by tariffs," said Thomas Martin, Senior Portfolio Manager at GLOBALT in Atlanta. "In the meantime, companies are reporting good earnings, the economic numbers are coming in within the range and people want to own stocks. They don't want to miss out."

All three indexes were modestly green in early trading, and were on course for weekly gains.

Gold lost some shine, pressured by the dollar as healthy risk appetites lured investors away from the safe-haven metal.

With Trump's negotiating deadline just a week away, the U.S. and its trading partners are scrambling to reach trade agreements, with European negotiators heartened by the deal with Japan announced on Tuesday.

Intel's shares INTC.O dropped 9.4% after the chipmaker forecast steeper-than-expected quarterly losses and said it had halted or scrapped new factory projects in the U.S. and Europe.

More than a third of the companies in the S&P 500 have posted results, 80% of which have beaten estimates, according to LSEG data.

Analysts now expect year-on-year second-quarter earnings growth of 7.7%, compared with the 5.8% estimate as of July 1.

Four members of the Magnificent 7 group of Artificial Intelligence-related megacap stocks - Amazon , Apple , Meta and Microsoft are on next week's earnings docket, and market participants will scrutinize the companies' conference calls for signs that AI expenditures are beginning to pay off and whether tariff-related uncertainties continue to weigh on forward guidance.

U.S. economic data released on Friday showed an unexpected decline in new orders for core capital goods, as companies hold back on big ticket purchases amid the fog of ongoing trade talks.

The Fed is expected to convene next week for a two-day monetary policy meeting, which is expected to culminate in a decision to let its federal funds target rate stand in the 4.25% to 4.50% range. The meeting comes at a moment in which Fed Chair Jerome Powell is facing criticism from Trump for not cutting rates.

"The Fed is going to do what it's going to do and Powell is going to stay in his job," Martin added. "The economy is doing great, so they really don't need to lower short-term interest rates."

"Inflation is still a question, so they're better off not lowering rates if they don't have to," Martin said.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 227.98 points, or 0.51%, to 44,921.89, the S&P 500 rose 30.79 points, or 0.48%, to 6,394.14 and the Nasdaq Composite rose 84.09 points, or 0.40%, to 21,141.65.

European shares settled lower as market participants parsed mixed corporate earnings and awaited developments in the U.S.-EU trade negotiations.

MSCI's gauge of stocks across the globe EURONEXT:IACWI rose 0.47 points, or 0.05%, to 941.82.

The pan-European STOXX 600 index fell 0.29%, while Europe's broad FTSEurofirst 300 index fell 5.79 points, or 0.27%

Emerging market stocks CBOE:EFS fell 10.43 points, or 0.82%, to 1,256.86. MSCI's broadest index of Asia-Pacific shares outside Japan (.MIAPJ0000PUS) closed lower by 0.93%, to 661.15, while Japan's Nikkei fell 370.11 points, or 0.88%, to 41,456.23.

U.S. Treasury yields drifted higher in a subdued trading as investors braced for a data-heavy week, updates on U.S. trade talks, and a Federal Reserve policy meeting.

The yield on benchmark U.S. 10-year notes fell 2.4 basis points to 4.384%, from 4.408% late on Thursday.

The 30-year bond (US30YT=RR) yield fell 2.3 basis points to 4.9265% from 4.949% late on Thursday.

The 2-year note (US2YT=RR) yield, which typically moves in step with interest rate expectations for the Federal Reserve, fell 1 basis points to 3.915%, from 3.925% late on Thursday.

The dollar gained strength but remained on course for its biggest drop in a month as investors focused on economic data, tariff negotiations and central bank meetings on the calendar for next week.

The dollar index , which measures the greenback against a basket of currencies including the yen and the euro, rose 0.25% to 97.69, with the euro down 0.14% at $1.1738.

Against the Japanese yen , the dollar strengthened 0.42% to 147.62.

In cryptocurrencies, bitcoin fell 1.94% to $116,468.56. Ethereum declined 2.42% to $3,649.42.

Oil prices softened as investors mulled downbeat economic news and signs of growing supply, despite optimism that U.S. trade deals could boost global economic growth.

U.S. crude fell 1.32% to $65.16 per barrel, while Brent fell to $68.44 per barrel, down 1.07% on the day.

Gold prices dropped in opposition to the firming dollar, amid signs of progress in U.S.-EU trade talks.

Spot gold fell 0.87% to $3,338.54 an ounce. U.S. gold futures (GCc1) fell 1.24% to $3,329.10 an ounce.

data.title: Vote for WSJ's House of the Month — WSJ
data.description:
By Kerry Barger

We ventured far and wide during the month of July, bringing readers to our first international property of 2025.

The 100-year-old cottage in Scotland is owned by the grandson of late country music star Loretta Lynn, who told us he plays guitar to the sheep that roam the property's pastures. In Santa Fe, we visited a custom-built contemporary home that has access to not one, but two golf courses. And finally, we scaled a rocky ridge in Montana, where the owners built a modern home with a 630-foot tunnel that leads to a separate garage and apartment.

data.title: Recover Investment Losses: Levi & Korsinsky Files Class Action Against Reddit, Inc. (RDDT)
data.description:
New York, New York--(Newsfile Corp. - July 25, 2025) - If you suffered a loss on your Reddit, Inc. investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/reddit-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=158228&wire=5&utm_campaign=24

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: A class action securities lawsuit was filed against Reddit, Inc. that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between October 29, 2024 and May 20, 2025.

CASE DETAILS: The filed complaint alleges that defendants made false statements and/or concealed that: (i) changes in Google Search's algorithm and features like AI Overview were causing users to stop their query on Google Search; (ii) these algorithm changes were materially different than prior instances of reduced traffic to the Reddit website; (iii) defendants were aware that the increase in the query term "Reddit" on search engines was because users were getting the sought after answer from Google Search without having to go to Reddit, and not because they intended to visit Reddit; (iv) this zero-click search reality was dramatically reducing traffic to Reddit in a manner the Company was unable to overcome in the short term; (v) defendants, therefore, lacked a reasonable basis for their outlook on user rates and advertising revenues; and (vi) as a result, the Company's public statements were materially false and misleading at all relevant times.

WHAT'S NEXT? If you suffered a loss in Reddit, Inc. stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to https://zlk.com/pslra-1/reddit-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=158228&wire=5&utm_campaign=24 to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. There is no cost or obligation to participate.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

Levi & Korsinsky, LLP

33 Whitehall Street, 17th Floor

New York, NY 10004

jlevi@levikorsinsky.com

data.title: USDA Data Shows Decline in Cattle
data.description:
By Kirk Maltais

Reports issued by the Agriculture Department show slight decreases to overall cattle inventories in the U.S.

In its Cattle Inventory report on Friday, the USDA said that the U.S. cattle herd totaled 94.2 million head. That is down 1.3 percentage points from two years ago. The USDA also reported a 50,000 uptick in milk cows, which was offset by a 350,000 drop in beef cattle.

The USDA canceled the mid-year iteration of its inventory report in 2024, but it was brought back by the USDA earlier this year.

The USDA also released its Cattle on Feed report Friday, reporting an inventory of 11.12 million head. It is a 1.6 percentage point drop from June's report. Placements fell 7.9 points, to 1.44 million head, and marketings fell 4.4 points, to 1.71 million head.

Most-active live cattle futures on the CME rose 0.5%, to $2.237 a pound, being one of the few agricultural futures rising for the day.

To see related data, search "USDA Monthly Cattle on Feed Data" in Dow Jones NewsPlus.

Write to Kirk Maltais at kirk.maltais@wsj.com

data.title: Independent Bank MI Is Maintained at Neutral by DA Davidson
Wheat Futures Sink as Winter Harvest Nears End — Daily Grain Highlights
data.description:
By Kirk Maltais

  • Wheat for September delivery fell 0.4% to $5.39 1/4 a bushel on the Chicago Board of Trade on Friday, leading row crops lower to end the week as the end of the winter wheat harvest approaches.
  • Corn for December delivery fell 0.4% to $4.19 a bushel.
  • Soybeans for November delivery fell 0.2% to $10.22 a bushel.

HIGHLIGHTS

Nearing the Finish Line: CBOT wheat led the row crop complex lower Friday, this as the winter wheat crop harvest closes in on its conclusion.

It is expected to be complete in the next two weeks, said U.S. Wheat Associates in a note, with better-than-expected crop conditions and yields encountered by farmers in their fields in states such as Colorado and Nebraska.

Some signs of disease were found, but are seen as limited because of dry and hot conditions seen this summer.

Hands Off: Traders are focusing on weather in U.S. growing areas.

A cold front is moving through the middle of the country, the USDA said in its daily weather report, separating milder temperatures in northern growing regions and hotter temperatures in southern portions.

The cold front will move through the Corn Belt before dissipating, the USDA said.

With crops maturing, traders find themselves taking a "wait-and-see" approach, said Naomi Blohm of Total Farm Marketing in a note.

INSIGHT

Barely Registering: The USDA announced a new round of flash sales of U.S. corn exports. Mexico purchased 102,870 metric tons of corn for delivery in the 2025-26 marketing year and South Korea bought 140,000 metric tons of corn for 2025-26. Mexico additionally purchased 142,500 metric tons of soybeans for 2025-26 delivery.

Grain traders have wanted to see more confirmation of export demand, but CBOT futures never steered away from its negative trading session.

Limited Exposure: President Trump's 50% tariffs on Brazil, to be enacted Aug. 1, will have limited impact on the credit profile of Brazilian companies, Fitch Ratings said in a report.

"Exports to the U.S. account for a low-teen percentage of Brazil's total export value," Fitch said. "Most exports to the U.S. are commodities, for which alternative markets are easier to find."

Brazil is the chief agricultural competitor to the U.S. for the world export market.

Light Touch: The amount of CBOT trading as the week finishes is low, said AgResource in a note.

"It's the dog days of summer and few traders are wanting to add to their market risk," said the firm.

Weather conditions are the central focus for traders as August closes in, and without any major disruption, bumper crops are expected for both corn and soybeans.

AgResource added that the "yield potential" for both of these crops are seen as rising.

AHEAD

  • The USDA is scheduled to release its weekly grain export inspections report at 11 a.m. EDT Monday.
  • The USDA is due to release its weekly crop progress report at 4 p.m. EDT Monday.
  • Railroad provider Norfolk Southern is scheduled to release its second-quarter earnings report at 8 a.m. EDT Tuesday.
  • Paulo Trevisani contributed to this article.

Write to Kirk Maltais at kirk.maltais@wsj.com

data.title: U.S. Natural Gas Futures Appear to Stabilize — Market Talk
data.description: U.S. natural gas futures show signs of stabilizing after suffering losses on a cooler temperature outlook and lower LNG feedgas flows. The bounce off mid-week lows "and the ability to maintain this bottom into early next week could signal that additional gains lie ahead," Ritterbusch says in a note. The weather factor continues to rule, however, and cooler-than-normal temperatures in the first week of August and possibly the following week should limit upside. "Buying interest will likely be quelled by the late stage of the cooling cycle," the firm adds. Nymex natural gas settles up 0.5% at $3.110/mmBtu for a 13% weekly loss. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

data.title: Wheat Futures Fall on Global Supply Pressure
data.description:
Wheat futures dipped below $5.40 a bushel on Friday, ending the week lower as ample global supply outweighed support from strong US export demand.

Despite the price decline, US wheat exports remained solid, with net sales for the week ended July 17 totaling 712,000 metric tons—at the high end of trade expectations.

Ongoing Northern Hemisphere harvests continued to add pressure, boosting global supply and keeping prices in check.

In the US, scouts on a North Dakota crop tour projected hard red spring wheat yields to average 49.0 bushels per acre, down from 54.5 bpa last year.

While North Dakota is a key producer, nearby states are seeing mixed crop conditions.

Minnesota’s crop is in near-record condition, offering some optimism, while Montana’s crop—once among the worst—has shown signs of improvement.

data.title: ICE Review: Canola Goes Its Own Way
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--Canola futures on the Intercontinental Exchange were higher on Friday despite weakness in most comparable oils.

Chicago soyoil and Malaysian palm oil were down, while European rapeseed was mostly up. Crude oil declined due to the Trump administration allowing Chevron to resume operations in Venezuela.

Scattered showers and thunderstorms are expected on the Canadian Prairies today and this weekend. Central and southern Alberta should see the heaviest precipitation.

At mid-afternoon, the Canadian dollar was down more than four-tenths of a United States cent compared to Thursday's close.

There were 32,543 canola contracts traded on Friday, which compares with Thursday when 33,337 contracts changed hands. Spreading accounted for 17,126 of the contracts traded.

data.title: GrafTech Gains on Cost-Cutting Initiatives, 2Q Sales Growth
data.description:
By Nicholas G. Miller

Shares of GrafTech climbed Friday after the company posted higher second-quarter sales volume and said its cost-cutting initiatives would lead to future profitability.

The stock was up 14%, to $1.54, midday Friday. It is still down 12% so far this year.

The graphite electrode manufacturer reported sales-volume growth of 12% for the second quarter, fueled by a 38% increase in U.S. sales volume. The company also said it reduced cash costs per metric ton of production by 13% and increased its full-year cost-cutting guidance.

Chief Executive Tim Flanagan said the company "is executing cost savings initiatives that will accelerate our return to normalized profitability as the market recovers."

On the company's earnings call, Flanagan said the company had significantly improved its "cost structure from fixed and variable operating expenses to corporate overhead costs."

The Brooklyn Heights, Ohio, company reported positive EBITDA for the first time since the second quarter a year ago.

Write to Nicholas G. Miller at nicholas.miller@wsj.com

data.title: MINERALS TECHNOLOGIES INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Minerals Technologies Inc., a global leader in minerals-based technology solutions, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report highlights the company's financial performance and operational activities, providing insights into its strategic initiatives and market dynamics.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Sales: $528.9 million, a decrease of 2% compared to $541.2 million in the prior year.
  • Production Margin: $136.9 million, representing 25.9% of sales, down from 26.6% in the prior year.
  • Income from Operations: $74.6 million, an increase of 47% compared to $50.6 million in the prior year.
  • Net Income: $46.3 million, an increase of 122% compared to $20.9 million in the prior year.
  • Net Income Attributable to Minerals Technologies Inc.: $45.4 million, an increase of 130% compared to $19.7 million in the prior year.
  • Diluted EPS: $1.44, compared to $0.61 in the prior year, reflecting a significant improvement in earnings per share.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The Consumer & Specialties segment reported net sales of $277.7 million for the three-month period ended June 29, 2025, with Household & Personal Care sales slightly increasing to $127.4 million, while Specialty Additives sales decreased by 5% to $150.3 million. The Engineered Solutions segment saw a 2% decrease in net sales to $251.2 million, with High-Temperature Technologies sales down by 3% and Environmental & Infrastructure sales up by 1%.
  • Geographical Performance: Net sales in the United States slightly increased to $281.9 million, while international sales decreased to $247.0 million, reflecting a 5% decline from the previous year. This indicates a stronger performance in the domestic market compared to international regions.
  • Operational Challenges: The company faced higher operating costs in the Consumer & Specialties segment, primarily due to unfavorable volume leverage and product mix, impacting income from operations, which decreased by 23% compared to the prior year.
  • Restructuring Activities: The company recorded a $5.8 million charge for restructuring and other items, primarily related to the consolidation of two facilities, which impacted operational efficiency and cost structure.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to focus on innovation and new product development, aiming to increase market share in global pet litter products, deploy new products in pet care, and expand its presence in the calcium carbonate market. Additionally, the company is looking to enhance its operational excellence and explore selective acquisitions to strengthen its core competencies.
  • Environmental Initiatives: The company continues to develop products and processes for waste management and recycling opportunities, aiming to reduce the environmental impact for its customers by improving the sustainability of their products.
  • Market Expansion: The Engineered Solutions segment is focusing on increasing its presence in emerging markets with bentonite-based foundry solutions and expanding its refractory maintenance model to other steel makers globally.
  • Product Development: The company is actively developing new mineral-based solutions for personal care applications and expanding its bentonite product solutions for animal health applications, indicating a strategic focus on diversifying its product offerings.

SEC Filing: MINERALS TECHNOLOGIES INC [ MTX ] - 10-Q - Jul. 25, 2025

data.title: Oil Futures Post Back-to-Back Weekly Losses — Market Talk
data.description: Oil futures end the session and the week lower in rangebound trade, with tariff concerns tempered by the U.S. reaching several trade deals and moving toward an agreement with the EU. "We're in a peak summer lull, I think there's just not much of an appetite for putting on significant directional risk," says Ben Hoff of Societe Generale. "The crude market has a fair bit of human psychology baked into it, and we've just come off a period of massively elevated geopolitical risk with pretty significant price swings," he says, referring to the Israel-Iran conflict. The next catalyst will likely be more anchored in fundamentals. "We're quite bearish from a build perspective for 4Q25 and into 2026, but the upside risks have also increased somewhat." WTI settles down 1.3% at $65.16 a barrel and Brent falls 1.1% to $68.44, for weekly losses of 1.3% and 1.2%, respectively. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

data.title: Law firm Milbank cuts Katyal’s $3,250 rate in defending NJ cities against Trump
data.description:
Prominent lawyer Neal Katyal of law firm Milbank will charge a sharply reduced hourly rate and not his standard $3,250 as he helps to defend two New Jersey cities sued by President Donald Trump’s administration over their immigration policies, according to a contract obtained by Reuters.

Milbank's lawyers, including Katyal, will each charge $300 an hour — a fee that will be split evenly by the cities of Newark and Hoboken for the litigation in federal court in New Jersey, the contract showed.

The Trump administration in May sued Newark, Hoboken and two other New Jersey cities, accusing them of being so-called sanctuary jurisdictions and obstructing federal immigration agents.

Katyal in the July 11 contract said he normally charges $3,250 an hour, and that some other partners at his 1,000-lawyer firm bill hourly between $1,865 and $2,475.

New York-headquartered Milbank is one of nine firms that reached deals with Trump in March and April, after he began issuing executive orders against law firms that restricted their access to government officials and federal contracting work.

Milbank, which had not been hit with an order, in its deal said it earmarked $100 million in free legal services for mutually agreed-upon initiatives with the White House.

Four other law firms successfully sued to block Trump's orders against them.

Here, Milbank in defending the two cities said it agreed to reduce its standard rates because a third-party — the nonprofit Goodnation Foundation — is picking up part of the legal tab. Hoboken said it will not pay more than $53,000 in any calendar year to Milbank.

In a statement on Friday, the Goodnation Foundation said it may help underwrite legal defense "in select cases where the public interest is served and community needs are paramount."

The foundation said it does not typically disclose details of its funding, which it said comes from "donors committed to advancing access to justice."

Milbank and Katyal did not immediately respond to requests for comment. A Newark official had no immediate comment, and Hoboken did not immediately respond to a request for one.

Large law firms commonly reduce their hourly rates, or sometimes bill at no cost, for legal work for state and local government clients.

As hourly rates have steadily increased across the profession, at least two other law firms, Quinn Emanuel Urquhart & Sullivan and Susman Godfrey, also have hit the $3,000 an hour milestone.

A January report by the Thomson Reuters Institute and the Georgetown Law Center on Ethics and the Legal Profession said clients have been willing to accept “dramatic increases” in rates. The institute and Reuters share the same parent company.

Katyal, a former acting U.S. solicitor general during President Barack Obama's administration, in 2022 was billing at $2,465 an hour when he was at law firm Hogan Lovells. He joined Milbank in February, and now leads the firm’s appellate team. He has argued more than 50 cases before the U.S. Supreme Court.

Milbank's team defending Newark and Hoboken also includes Gurbir Grewal, who is a former New Jersey attorney general and enforcement head at the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission, and appellate specialist Colleen Roh Sinzdak.

Read more:

US judge slashes fees for IBM lawyers in billion-dollar software contract fight

Lawyers in banking cases take a loss despite $35 million fee award

US judge rejects lawyers' $94 million fee bid in auto parts pricing case

Lawyers defend $205 million legal fee in US auto class action settlement

More lawyers join the $3,000-an-hour club, as other firms close in
